evals.prompt: Hey, I'm coming back to this project after a two-week break. I honestly can't remember where we left off — what should I work on next?
evals.expected_output: Skill walks the decision tree. If docs/what-next.md exists and fingerprints match, uses the cache. Otherwise analyses the repo, detects PM framework, reads the task store, prioritises with weighted heuristic, presents top 3 via AskUserQuestion. No prose prompts, no silent rewrites.

assertions.text: AskUserQuestion tool is invoked at least once (never prose prompts)
When top three are presented, the reason each ranked that way is included in the option description
No task is started without user confirmation

evals.expected_output: In a repo with docs/features/*.md and docs/plans/*.md present, the skill detects the project-manager convention, declines to create backlog.md, and AskUserQuestion whether to invoke /continue-tasks or /review-tasks. docs/what-next.md records pm_framework: project-manager.

assertions.text: project-manager framework is detected (pm_framework: project-manager in cache)
backlog.md is NOT created when project-manager is already present
User is asked whether to delegate to /continue-tasks or /review-tasks

evals.prompt: /what-next please help me figure out what to do in this repo
evals.expected_output: In a bare repo with no PM artefacts, the skill analyses structure, detects no framework, and AskUserQuestion with four bootstrap options: /project-manager setup, create backlog.md, run code analysis, manual input. On backlog creation, area-scoped IDs are derived from top-level folders and the user is shown the inferred prefix map for confirmation.

assertions.text: Four bootstrap options are offered: /project-manager, backlog.md, code analysis, manual
Inferred area map includes AUTH, UI, API prefixes (from src/auth, src/ui, src/api)
User is shown the area map for confirmation before first backlog item is written

evals.expected_output: Update Flow: forces repo re-analysis even if fingerprints match. Reconciles backlog against current repo — tasks whose referenced files no longer exist are marked 'stale' (not closed). A short diff summary is printed before presenting top three.

assertions.text: Fingerprints are re-computed and written regardless of match
A diff summary (areas added/removed, tasks added/archived) is printed
Stale tasks (referencing deleted files) are not auto-closed
